infobox.value: (**NFR**) is the United States [National Film Preservation Board](/wiki/National_Film_Preservation_Board "National Film Preservation Board")'s (NFPB) collection of [films](/wiki/Film "Film") selected for [preservation](/wiki/Preservation_(library_and_archival_science) "Preservation (library and archival science)"), each for its cultural, historical and aesthetic contributions since the NFPB's inception in 1988.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Selection of films for preservation in the US Library of Congress

National Film Registry

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Former logo for the National Film Registry. | |
| Founded | * 1988; 38 years ago (1988) * [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |
| Type | Film preservation |
| Website | [loc.gov/programs/national-film-preservation-board/film-registry](http://loc.gov/programs/national-film-preservation-board/film-registry) |

The **National Film Registry** (**NFR**) is the United States [National Film Preservation Board](/wiki/National_Film_Preservation_Board "National Film Preservation Board")'s (NFPB) collection of [films](/wiki/Film "Film") selected for [preservation](/wiki/Preservation_(library_and_archival_science) "Preservation (library and archival science)"), each for its cultural, historical and aesthetic contributions since the NFPB's inception in 1988.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Film_Registry&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Throughout the 1980s, several prominent filmmakers and industry personalities in the United States, such as [Frank Capra](/wiki/Frank_Capra "Frank Capra") and [Martin Scorsese](/wiki/Martin_Scorsese "Martin Scorsese"), advocated for Congress to enact a film preservation bill in order to avoid commercial modifications (such as [pan and scan](/wiki/Pan_and_scan "Pan and scan") and editing for television) of classic films, which they saw as negative. In response to the controversy over the [colorization of originally black and white films](/wiki/Film_colorization#Entertainment_make-overs "Film colorization") in the decade specifically, Representatives [Robert J. Mrazek](/wiki/Robert_J._Mrazek "Robert J. Mrazek") and [Sidney R. Yates](/wiki/Sidney_R._Yates "Sidney R. Yates") introduced the [National Film Preservation Act](/wiki/National_Film_Preservation_Act "National Film Preservation Act") of 1988, which established the National Film Registry, its purpose, and the criteria for selecting films for preservation.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The Act was passed and the NFR's mission was subsequently reauthorized by further [acts of Congress](/wiki/Act_of_Congress "Act of Congress") in 1992, 1996, 2005, 2008 and 2016.[[4]](#cite_note-NFPASigned-4)[[5]](#cite_note-ActsCongress-5)[[6]](#cite_note-PL110336-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) The National Film Preservation Board's mission, to which the NFR contributes, is to ensure the survival, conservation, and increased public availability of America's film heritage.[[8]](#cite_note-8) The 1996 law also created the non-profit [National Film Preservation Foundation](/wiki/National_Film_Preservation_Foundation "National Film Preservation Foundation") which, although affiliated with the NFPB, raises money from the [private sector](/wiki/Private_sector "Private sector").[[9]](#cite_note-NFPFPrivate-9)

## Selection criteria

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Film_Registry&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Selection criteria")]

> Taken together, the ... films in the National Film Registry represent a stunning range of American filmmaking—including Hollywood features, documentaries, avant-garde and amateur productions, films of regional interest, ethnic, animated, and short film subjects—all deserving recognition, preservation and access by future generations. As we begin this new millennium, the registry stands among the finest summations of American cinema's wondrous first century.

—Doctor [James H. Billington](/wiki/James_H._Billington "James H. Billington"), [Librarian of Congress](/wiki/Librarian_of_Congress "Librarian of Congress").[[10]](#cite_note-Milli25Films-10)

The NFPB adds to the NFR up to 25 "culturally, historically or aesthetically significant films" each year in December, showcasing the range and diversity of [American film heritage](/wiki/Cinema_of_the_United_States "Cinema of the United States") to increase awareness for its preservation.[[10]](#cite_note-Milli25Films-10) A film becomes eligible for inclusion ten years after its original release.[[11]](#cite_note-10YearsNYT-11) For the first selection in 1989, the public nominated almost 1,000 films for consideration. Members of the NFPB then developed individual ballots of possible films for inclusion. The ballots were tabulated into a list of 25 films that was then modified by [Librarian of Congress](/wiki/Librarian_of_Congress "Librarian of Congress") [James H. Billington](/wiki/James_H._Billington "James H. Billington") and his staff at the Library for the final selection.[[10]](#cite_note-Milli25Films-10) Since 1997, members of the public have been able to nominate up to 50 films a year for the NFPB and Librarian to consider,[[12]](#cite_note-PublicNominate-12) with an August submission deadline.

The NFR, collaborating with other archives, studios, copyright holders and individuals,[[13]](#cite_note-13) includes films ranging from Hollywood classics to [orphan films](/wiki/Orphan_film "Orphan film").[[14]](#cite_note-14) A film is not required to be [feature-length](/wiki/Feature_film "Feature film"), nor is it required to have been [theatrically](/wiki/Movie_theater "Movie theater") released in the traditional sense. The Registry contains [newsreels](/wiki/Newsreel "Newsreel"), [silent films](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film"), student films, [experimental films](/wiki/Experimental_film "Experimental film"), [short films](/wiki/Short_film "Short film"), [music videos](/wiki/Music_video "Music video"), films out of [copyright](/wiki/Copyright "Copyright") protection or in the [public domain](/wiki/Public_domain "Public domain"), [film serials](/wiki/Serial_film "Serial film"), [home movies](/wiki/Home_movies "Home movies"), documentaries, animation and [independent films](/wiki/Independent_film "Independent film"). As of the 2025 listing, there are 925 films in the Registry.

### Age of Registry selections

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Film_Registry&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Age of Registry selections")]

The oldest film in the registry, *[Newark Athlete](/wiki/Newark_Athlete "Newark Athlete")*, was released in 1891, while the most recent, *[The Grand Budapest Hotel](/wiki/The_Grand_Budapest_Hotel "The Grand Budapest Hotel")*, was released in 2014.

### Time span from release to selection

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Film_Registry&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Time span from release to selection")]

Released in 1895 and 1896, and selected in December 2024 and January 2026, *[Annabelle Serpentine Dance](/wiki/Annabelle_Serpentine_Dance "Annabelle Serpentine Dance")* and *[The Tramp and the Dog](/wiki/The_Tramp_and_the_Dog "The Tramp and the Dog")* jointly experienced the longest wait, at 129 years each, while *[Raging Bull](/wiki/Raging_Bull "Raging Bull")*, released theatrically in New York City, Los Angeles and Toronto on November 14, 1980, and inducted in October 1990, holds the record for the shortest delay, having been inducted slightly shy of the 10-year minimum.[[30]](#cite_note-1990Add-30) Only eight other films have been inducted at the 10-year mark: *[Do the Right Thing](/wiki/Do_the_Right_Thing "Do the Right Thing")*,[[55]](#cite_note-66) *[Goodfellas](/wiki/Goodfellas "Goodfellas")*,[[56]](#cite_note-67) *[Toy Story](/wiki/Toy_Story "Toy Story")*,[[57]](#cite_note-68)[[58]](#cite_note-69) *[Fargo](/wiki/Fargo_(1996_film) "Fargo (1996 film)")*,[[59]](#cite_note-70) *[13 Lakes](/wiki/13_Lakes "13 Lakes")*,[[60]](#cite_note-71) *[Freedom Riders](/wiki/Freedom_Riders_(film) "Freedom Riders (film)")*,[[61]](#cite_note-72) *[12 Years a Slave](/wiki/12_Years_a_Slave_(film) "12 Years a Slave (film)")* and *[20 Feet from Stardom](/wiki/20_Feet_from_Stardom "20 Feet from Stardom")*.[[62]](#cite_note-73)

## Franchises with multiple entries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Film_Registry&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Franchises with multiple entries")]

While most film franchises have at most one entry in the registry, some have two or more. These include sequels, remakes, or adaptations of the same source material.

* [Ben-Hur](/wiki/Ben-Hur:_A_Tale_of_the_Christ "Ben-Hur: A Tale of the Christ"): *[Ben-Hur: A Tale of the Christ](/wiki/Ben-Hur:_A_Tale_of_the_Christ_(1925_film) "Ben-Hur: A Tale of the Christ (1925 film)")*, *[Ben-Hur](/wiki/Ben-Hur_(1959_film) "Ben-Hur (1959 film)")*
* *[The Crowd](/wiki/The_Crowd_(1928_film) "The Crowd (1928 film)")* and its sequel *[Our Daily Bread](/wiki/Our_Daily_Bread_(1934_film) "Our Daily Bread (1934 film)")*
* [Dracula](/wiki/Dracula "Dracula"): *[Dracula](/wiki/Dracula_(1931_English-language_film) "Dracula (1931 English-language film)")* (English-language version), *[Dracula](/wiki/Dracula_(1931_Spanish-language_film) "Dracula (1931 Spanish-language film)")* (Spanish-language version), *[Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein](/wiki/Abbott_and_Costello_Meet_Frankenstein "Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein")*
* [The Fall of the House of Usher](/wiki/The_Fall_of_the_House_of_Usher "The Fall of the House of Usher"): *[The Fall of the House of Usher](/wiki/The_Fall_of_the_House_of_Usher_(1928_American_film) "The Fall of the House of Usher (1928 American film)")*, *[House of Usher](/wiki/House_of_Usher_(film) "House of Usher (film)")*
* [Frankenstein](/wiki/Frankenstein "Frankenstein"): *[Frankenstein](/wiki/Frankenstein_(1931_film) "Frankenstein (1931 film)")*, *[Bride of Frankenstein](/wiki/Bride_of_Frankenstein "Bride of Frankenstein")*, *[Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein](/wiki/Abbott_and_Costello_Meet_Frankenstein "Abbott and Costello Meet Frankenstein")*, *[Young Frankenstein](/wiki/Young_Frankenstein "Young Frankenstein")*
* [The Front Page](/wiki/The_Front_Page "The Front Page"): *[The Front Page](/wiki/The_Front_Page_(1931_film) "The Front Page (1931 film)")*, *[His Girl Friday](/wiki/His_Girl_Friday "His Girl Friday")*
* [The Godfather](/wiki/The_Godfather_(film_series) "The Godfather (film series)")*: [The Godfather](/wiki/The_Godfather "The Godfather")*, *[The Godfather Part II](/wiki/The_Godfather_Part_II "The Godfather Part II")*
* [Imitation of Life](/wiki/Imitation_of_Life_(novel) "Imitation of Life (novel)"): *[Imitation of Life](/wiki/Imitation_of_Life_(1934_film) "Imitation of Life (1934 film)")* (1934), *[Imitation of Life](/wiki/Imitation_of_Life_(1959_film) "Imitation of Life (1959 film)")* (1959)
* [The Philadelphia Story](/wiki/The_Philadelphia_Story_(play) "The Philadelphia Story (play)"): *[The Philadelphia Story](/wiki/The_Philadelphia_Story_(film) "The Philadelphia Story (film)")*, *[High Society](/wiki/High_Society_(1956_film) "High Society (1956 film)")*
* [Philip Marlowe](/wiki/Philip_Marlowe "Philip Marlowe"): *[The Big Sleep](/wiki/The_Big_Sleep_(1946_film) "The Big Sleep (1946 film)")*, *[The Long Goodbye](/wiki/The_Long_Goodbye_(film) "The Long Goodbye (film)")*
* [Star Wars](/wiki/Star_Wars "Star Wars"): *[Star Wars](/wiki/Star_Wars_(film) "Star Wars (film)")*, *[The Empire Strikes Back](/wiki/The_Empire_Strikes_Back "The Empire Strikes Back")*, *[Return of the Jedi](/wiki/Return_of_the_Jedi "Return of the Jedi")*
* [Terminator](/wiki/Terminator_(franchise) "Terminator (franchise)"): *[The Terminator](/wiki/The_Terminator "The Terminator")*, *[Terminator 2: Judgment Day](/wiki/Terminator_2:_Judgment_Day "Terminator 2: Judgment Day")*

## Directors with multiple entries (two or more)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=National_Film_Registry&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Directors with multiple entries (two or more)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/04/John_Ford_1946.jpg/250px-John_Ford_1946.jpg)](/wiki/File:John_Ford_1946.jpg)

[Academy Award–winning director](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Director "Academy Award for Best Director") [John Ford](/wiki/John_Ford "John Ford") has the most entries with 11 films.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/94/Hitchcock%2C_Alfred_02.jpg/250px-Hitchcock%2C_Alfred_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hitchcock,_Alfred_02.jpg)

Master of Suspense [Alfred Hitchcock](/wiki/Alfred_Hitchcock "Alfred Hitchcock"), responsible for inductees ranging from the 1940 [Academy Award for Best Picture](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Picture "Academy Award for Best Picture") winner *[Rebecca](/wiki/Rebecca_(1940_film) "Rebecca (1940 film)")* to 1963's *[The Birds](/wiki/The_Birds_(film) "The Birds (film)")*.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8b/Welles-Magnificent-Ambersons-Pub-A16.jpg/250px-Welles-Magnificent-Ambersons-Pub-A16.jpg)](/wiki/File:Welles-Magnificent-Ambersons-Pub-A16.jpg)

[Orson Welles](/wiki/Orson_Welles "Orson Welles"), acclaimed filmmaker behind inductees *[Citizen Kane](/wiki/Citizen_Kane "Citizen Kane")* (1941), *[The Magnificent Ambersons](/wiki/The_Magnificent_Ambersons_(film) "The Magnificent Ambersons (film)")* (1942), *[The Lady from Shanghai](/wiki/The_Lady_from_Shanghai "The Lady from Shanghai")* (1948) and *[Touch of Evil](/wiki/Touch_of_Evil "Touch of Evil")* (1958).

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/59/Stanley_Kubrick_in_Dr._Strangelove_Trailer_%284%29_Cropped.jpg/250px-Stanley_Kubrick_in_Dr._Strangelove_Trailer_%284%29_Cropped.jpg)](/wiki/File:Stanley_Kubrick_in_Dr._Strangelove_Trailer_(4)_Cropped.jpg)

[Stanley Kubrick](/wiki/Stanley_Kubrick "Stanley Kubrick"), master [auteur](/wiki/Auteur "Auteur") responsible for inductees ranging from 1957's *[Paths of Glory](/wiki/Paths_of_Glory "Paths of Glory")* to his 1980 adaptation of [Stephen King](/wiki/Stephen_King "Stephen King")'s *[The Shining](/wiki/The_Shining_(film) "The Shining (film)")*.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c2/Dave_Fleischer_-_Motion_Picture_Herald%2C_Feb_3_1940_page_29_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Dave_Fleischer_-_Motion_Picture_Herald%2C_Feb_3_1940_page_29_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dave_Fleischer_-_Motion_Picture_Herald,_Feb_3_1940_page_29_(cropped).jpg)

Aside from animated shorts/features by producer [Walt Disney](/wiki/Walt_Disney "Walt Disney") alongside multiple directors, animator [Dave Fleischer](/wiki/Dave_Fleischer "Dave Fleischer") has the most animated titles on the Registry with four (some featuring timeless characters [Koko the Clown](/wiki/Koko_the_Clown "Koko the Clown"), [Betty Boop](/wiki/Betty_Boop "Betty Boop") and [Popeye](/wiki/Popeye "Popeye")).
